stories.title: The U.S. fights to reopen the Strait of Hormuz as UAE says it's attacked by Iran
stories.description: The U.S. military said it battled Iranian forces and sank six small boats as it moved to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, while the United Arab Emirates said Iran fired missiles and drones its way.

stories.title: Trump says U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships through the Strait. And, key Senate races to watch
stories.description: President Trump announced that the U.S will "guide" stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz. And, Democrats face an uphill battle to gain Senate control. Here are the key races to watch.

stories.title: Trump says the U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships from the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The United States will launch an effort on Monday to "guide" stranded ships from the Iran-gripped Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump said, as two ships around the strait reported attacks.

stories.title: US strikes Iranian fast boats as Iran attacks UAE oil facility
stories.description: Shipping company Maersk says one of its US-flagged commercial vessels has successfully exited the Strait of Hormuz under US military protection.

stories.title: Israeli attacks kill dozens within days in Lebanon despite ceasefire
stories.description: The health ministry says 110 people have been killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon since Thursday.

stories.title: Some Iranians fear the regime is now more entrenched - and ready for revenge
stories.description: Ordinary Iranians tell of worry about increased repression by authorities after the war is over.

stories.title: Thirteen killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, health ministry says
stories.description: Four women and a child are among the dead, as fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah continues despite a ceasefire.

stories.title: British couple jailed in Iran: 'We're likely to be here for a long time'
stories.description: Lindsay and Craig Foreman are facing the reality of a 10-year prison sentence following their arrest in Iran while on a motorcycle tour last year.

stories.title: Israeli 'double-tap' strike kills three rescue workers in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: They are among nine people killed in successive attacks on Tuesday, which Israel's military says targeted a Hezbollah commander.

stories.title: Former US officials criticise Pentagon silence on deadly Iran school attack
stories.description: In the two months since the deadly strike, the US defence department has said only that the incident is under investigation.

stories.title: Why and how is US blockading Iranian ports in Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

stories.title: Watch: There's a ceasefire in Lebanon - but the fighting hasn't stopped
stories.description: Israel and Hezbollah are still fighting in Lebanon despite a US-brokered ceasefire that began 12 days ago.

stories.title: Did Trump save eight Iranian women from execution?
stories.description: The US president says he saved eight Iranian women from execution, but Iran says otherwise.

stories.title: Middle East war live: Saudi Arabia calls for de-escalation after US-Iran clashes
stories.description: Saudi Arabia called on Tuesday to de-escalate the conflict in the Middle East after Iran and the United States traded fire, rattling a fragile ceasefire. The UN Security Council is set to hold a closed-door meeting on Lebanon later in the day. Follow our liveblog for the latest d...

stories.title: US and UAE report Iran attacks as military pushes to reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: US forces opened a passage through the Strait of Hormuz on Monday after sinking Iranian boats and escorting merchant ships, as fresh attacks struck vessels and facilities in the United Arab Emirates. The escalation has raised fears over global energy supplies and risks derailing ...

stories.title: Can solar cars become mainstream?
stories.description: The energy crisis caused by the Iran war has been a wake-up call and prompted governments to try and speed up a shift to electric cars. Could solar cars be next? Although they have been around for decades, solar-powered vehicles have largely remained experimental due to technical...

stories.title: Opaque nature of US-Iran shadow war: Are they 'dancing their way out of a minefield' toward a deal?
stories.description: François Picard welcomes Peter Apps, Global Defence Commentator, who writes a bi-weekly column for Reuters on national security, conflict, international affairs, and technology. Rather than casting the current state of US–Iran tensions as a binary shift between war and peace, App...

stories.title: War of words: Trump, Iran trade claims of missile strikes in the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz escalated Monday after US President Donald Trump announced Washington was beginning "Project Freedom," to help "guide" ships stranded by Iran's closure of the crucial waterway. Iran said the US had "disregarded" its warning shots and that it had s...

stories.title: 'America has confirmed everything that Iran has been telling its population', analyst says
stories.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Emma Salisbury, Senior Research Fellow on the National Security Programme at the Foreign Policy Institute, says that "America has confirmed everything that Iran has been telling its population", explaining that "the Iranian regime tell their p...

stories.title: Fujairah oil zone hit by fire after drone attack as UAE says it intercepted Iran missiles
stories.description: Fire ​broke out at a major UAE oil industry zone on Monday after a ​drone attack originating from Iran, authorities said, as the Gulf state's military separately intercepted three Iranian missiles over its waters and a fourth crashed into the sea. Hoda Abdel Hamid reports for FRA...

stories.title: Iran fires 'warning shots' as US Navy says its ships entered Gulf via Hormuz
stories.description: The ceasefire in the Iran war abruptly faced its most perilous moment Monday after the United States began trying to open the Strait of Hormuz to allow hundreds of stranded commercial ships sail out. The United Arab Emirates said it came under attack for the first time since the ...

stories.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 67 as Hormuz crisis deepens?
stories.description: The UAE says it intercepted Iranian missiles after a suspected drone attack, with no comment from Iran.

stories.title: Iran says US military killed five civilians in attacks on passenger boats
stories.description: Iranian commander says US military attacked two passenger boats, not IRGC vessels, in the Strait of Hormuz on Monday.

stories.title: ‘Dangerous escalation’: World condemns Iran after attacks on UAE
stories.description: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait and Bahrain denounce strikes, as Germany, UK and Canada urge Iran to return to talks.

stories.title: Oil prices surge as violence flares in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Brent crude rises nearly 6 percent as soaring tensions in waterway push ceasefire to the brink.

stories.title: Iran war live: Washington, Tehran trade threats over Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Israeli attacks have killed 2,696 people in Lebanon since March 2, the country's Health Ministry reports.

stories.title: Hormuz tensions push ceasefire to the brink as Trump threatens Iran
stories.description: US military says it is encouraging ships to pass through Hormuz Strait, but Iran says it is in full control of waterway.

stories.title: Europeans, Canadian PM discuss security amid Trump’s threats
stories.description: YEREVAN: America’s Nato allies put a brave face on Monday on a US decision to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany, but warned it was proof Europe needs to step up for its own security, and fast. US President Donald Trump cast fresh doubts over the future of the alliance as he anno...

stories.title: Hezbollah fighters, Israeli troops clash in Lebanon
stories.description: • Aoun says security deal must come before meeting with Netanyahu• Hezbollah chief opposes negotiations until war stops BEIRUT: Hezbollah said its forces clashed with Israeli soldiers on Monday in south Lebanon near the border where the troops are still operating, despite a cease...

stories.title: Missiles fly as Hormuz tensions come to a head
stories.description: • US says destroyed ‘several Iranian boats’ after Iran claims strike on warship in Hormuz• Trump warns Iranian forces will be ‘blown off the face of earth’ if they try to intercept US vessels• Fire at UAE port after drone attack, three Indian nationals hurt; Korean ship damaged i...

stories.title: Energy crisis fuels calls to cut methane emissions
stories.description: PARIS: World officials pushed on Monday for faster action to reduce methane emissions from the fossil fuel sector, arguing it would both help slow climate change and boost energy security as the Middle East war chokes off supply. Using its role as rotating chair of the Group of S...

stories.title: Fujairah oil zone hit by drone attack as UAE says it intercepted Iranian missiles
stories.description: Fire broke out at a major UAE oil industry zone on Monday after a drone attack originating from Iran, ​authorities said, as the Gulf state’s military separately intercepted three Iranian ‌missiles over its waters and a fourth crashed into the sea. Civil defence teams were deploye...

stories.title: Support among American Jews for US-led war on Iran declines: survey
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Public opinion within parts of the American-Jewish community on the US-led war against Iran has shown a noticeable shift, with support easing and opposition rising over time, according to a recent survey. While different polls focus on different segments of the commun...

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: PM Modi condemns attacks on the UAE, which resulted in injuries to three Indian nationals
stories.description: U.S. President Donald ​Trump launched “Project Freedom” seeking to open the Strait of Hormuz to shipping, prompting Iran to lash out

stories.title: South Korea to probe ship fire in Strait of Hormuz, Trump blames Iranian attack
stories.description: “The exact cause of the accident would be figured out after the vessel is towed and ‌its damage is assessed,” South Korea’s Foreign Ministry said in a ‌statement

stories.title: First Russian oil arrives in Japan since Iran war: media
stories.description: Japan, which depends on West Asia for around 95% of its oil imports, has tried to diversify sources of energy procurement since the outbreak of war on February 28 saw Tehran effectively shut the Strait

stories.title: Trump launches ‘Project Freedom’ to open Strait of Hormuz, Iran strikes ships, UAE port
stories.description: Mr. Trump announced his new “Project Freedom” mission on social media overnight to enable stuck ships to travel through the strait

stories.title: Three Indians injured after fire breaks out in UAE’s Fujairah due to drone attack
stories.description: Three Indian nationals sustained injuries after the fire broke out on the Fujairah Petroleum Industries Zone, following a drone attack from Iran, local media reported

stories.title: UAE Defence Ministry says four cruise missiles fired from Iran
stories.description: Authorities ​in the ‌United Arab Emirates’ Fujairah ​said ⁠on May 4, that a ‌fire broke out ‌at ‌the Fujairah ⁠Oil ⁠Industry Zone following what they ​described ‌as a drone attack originating from ‌Iran.
